title: Canada’s Online Streaming Act: Background and Issues for Congress
summary: In April 2023, Canada enacted the Online Streaming Act, amending Canada’s Broadcasting Act to allow the Canadian Radio-Television and Telecommunications Commission (CRTC) to regulate entities that broadcast video or music through social media (e.g., Meta’s Facebook) or online streaming services (e.g., Amazon Prime, Disney+, Netflix, Spotify, YouTube). The CRTC is an independent quasi-judicial tribunal that...

title: Sustaining Agile Operations
summary: This report analyzes emerging energy and water technologies that could improve operational agility in contested environments. It evaluates advances in portable power, storage, and purification to enhance sustainability and reduce logistics risk.

title: The Nuanced Relationship Between Public Opinion and Foreign Policy
summary: Public sentiment is something decision-makers must contend with, especially in democratic states. However, the extent to which public opinion shapes foreign policy varies. Three Council of Councils experts in France, India, and Nigeria reflect on how public opinion does—and sometimes does not—affect foreign policy decisions.

title: A New Series of CFR Playbooks Outlines How U.S. Policymakers Can Navigate Foreign Policy Crisis Scenarios
summary: In a new collection of reports from the Wachenheim Center for Peace and Security at the Council on Foreign Relations (CFR), authors Peter J. Quaranto, Cecily Brewer, F. David Diaz, and Jason Ladnier provide expert recommendations for how to respond to common contingency types. The Crisis Response Playbooks cover how to navigate insurgencies, coups, gray […]

title: American Crisis Management for a New Era
summary: The United States lacks a rigorous, routinized approach to managing foreign policy crises. The Wachenheim Center for Peace and Security has developed a set of Crisis Response Playbooks and a Crisis Management Checklist to serve as quick-reference guides for policy planners during the immediate pre-crisis and active crisis phases, when bandwidth is limited and decisions are most consequential. The playbooks are...

title: The U.S. Is About to Design an AI Regulator. Here’s How to Get It Right.
summary: The AI industry and the administration could soon converge on a FINRA-style regulator for frontier AI. Its ultimate viability and effectiveness will be decided by design choices being made right now.

title: Somalia
summary: Instability and humanitarian crises have beset Somalia for decades. After the authoritarian Siad Barre regime fell amid civil war in 1991, the country was widely viewed for years as a “failed state.” The formation of a new federal government in 2012 was a turning point, and Somalia, with international support, made halting progress against a violent Islamist insurgency led by Al Shabaab. title: Libya and U.S. Policy
summary: Fifteen years after a 2011 uprising and U.S.-backed military intervention that toppled longtime authoritarian leader Muammar al Qadhafi, Libya has yet to make a transition to stable governing arrangements. Elections and diplomacy have produced a series of interim governments, but militias, local leaders, and subnational coalitions backed by competing foreign patrons have remained powerful. title: National Level Exercises: History, Authorities, and Congressional Considerations
summary: Every two years, the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) conducts a National Level Exercise (NLE) to evaluate the nation’s progress toward preparedness for catastrophic events. title: China's strategic petroleum reserve as a geoeconomic tool
summary: Chinese policy around the use of their SPR as a geoeconomic tool, particularly in crisis scenarios, is not well understood. This project investigates potential ways for China to do so, but not whether China actually intends to use it in this way.

title: Slowing Data Center Construction Signals the Need for Pro-Growth Reforms

title: June's Inflation Relief Was a Peace Dividend
summary: American families finally saw some financial relief in June as prices at the pump fell. summary: Senate Bill 1160 reduced utilization review (UR) requirements for some treatments during the first 30 days after a work-related injury. The authors examined effects on UR processes and medical treatment in California’s workers’ compensation system.

title: Budget Reconciliation Measures Enacted into Law Since 1980
summary: The budget reconciliation process is an optional procedure that operates as an adjunct to the budget resolution process established by the Congressional Budget Act of 1974. The chief purpose of the reconciliation process is to enhance Congress’s ability to change current law in order to bring revenue, spending, and debt limit levels into conformity with the policies of the annual budget resolution.
